name: Anterior
description: AI clinical-review software for health-plan utilization management and prior authorization. Its 'Florence' AI agent prepares and reasons over medical records to support payer clinical-review decisions, with KLAS Research-validated 99.24% clinical accuracy in live production and deployments covering approximately 50 million lives.

attributes.problemStatement: Prior authorization and utilization review are slow, expensive, and administratively burdensome for payers, providers, and patients, delaying care and driving denial-appeal costs.
attributes.productDescription: Enterprise AI platform ('Florence') that prepares medical records and performs clinical reasoning to accelerate and support payer prior-authorization and utilization-review decisions, independently validated by KLAS Research for clinical accuracy.

founders.name: Abdel Mahmoud
founders.background: Physician who pursued a master's degree in computer science; led the company through its June 2024 Series A and February 2026 Series B.

name: Freed
description: AI medical scribe for independent and community-practice clinicians, converting patient visits into structured clinical notes with an EHR integration, plus a coding assistant and an AI front-desk receptionist product. Reports 26,000+ clinicians, 1,300+ clinics, and 32.6 million patient visits transcribed in 2025.

disruptionMechanisms: Product-led growth into an underserved segment
attributes.problemStatement: Clinicians in independent and community practices spend hours per day on clinical documentation, and are underserved by enterprise-focused AI scribes built for large health systems.
attributes.productDescription: Self-serve AI medical scribe ($39/month) plus a coding assistant and an AI front-desk receptionist product ($149/month), purpose-built for independent and community clinical practices.

founders.name: Erez Druk
founders.background: Former Meta engineer; co-founded Freed after observing his wife's, Dr. Gabi Meckler, documentation burden as a clinician.

founders.name: Andrey Bannikov
founders.background: Former Meta engineer; co-built Freed's transcription and clinical-note technology.

name: Counsel Health
description: AI-native, physician-supervised virtual primary-care company. A medical-grade AI chat interface triages members and escalates to real physicians via chat or video within minutes; expanding into chronic-condition management in 2026. Reports more than 100,000 members served.

productCategoryPath: AI triage and virtual primary care
attributes.businessModel: Consumer subscription plus B2B/PMPY enterprise contracts
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with employed/contracted physician network
attributes.problemStatement: Access to timely, trustworthy primary-care guidance is slow and expensive, and prior AI-triage products have struggled to convert triage into durable, safe physician-guided care.
attributes.productDescription: AI chat-based medical triage that escalates to physician chat or video visits within minutes ($29/visit or $199/year unlimited), supervised by an in-house physician group, expanding into chronic-condition management.

founders.name: Muthu Alagappan
founders.background: Stanford-trained MD; prior chief medical officer at Notable Health for approximately four years; attending physician at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center and UCSF Medical Center.

name: Summer Health
description: Text-first pediatric care service: parents message a board-certified pediatrician and typically get a response within 15 minutes, including prescriptions when appropriate. Subscription is $45/month, with a newer $299/year pediatric biomarker blood-panel add-on. No follow-on financing publicly reported since its April 2024 Series A.

attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with contracted pediatrician network
attributes.problemStatement: Parents lack fast, trustworthy access to pediatric medical guidance outside standard office hours, often resorting to costly emergency-room visits for non-emergency concerns.
attributes.productDescription: Text-messaging-based pediatric care platform with a 15-minute response SLA, prescription capability, and expansions into lactation support, sleep training, and developmental-milestone tracking.

founders.name: Ellen DaSilva
founders.background: Early employee (#8) at Hims & Hers, serving as Head of Strategic Partnerships through IPO; prior business-operations leadership at Twitter.

founders.name: Matthew Woo
founders.background: Prior consulting at Simon-Kucher & Partners; 9+ years in product roles at Meetup, Yo, Intercom, UJET, and Meta/WhatsApp.

name: Millie
description: Tech-enabled, midwife-led maternity and gynecology clinic model combining in-person and virtual care across the full maternity journey (conception through postpartum), accepting major insurance. Operates clinics in Berkeley and South Bay, California, with a San Jose location planned. Correct domain: millieclinic.com (roster hint milliehealth.com belongs to an unrelated company).

founderArchetypes: Clinician-adjacent founder with personal health crisis motivation
attributes.goToMarket: Direct-to-consumer plus payer contracting, physical clinic locations
attributes.problemStatement: U.S. maternal-health outcomes are poor relative to peer countries, with significant preventable morbidity and mortality, and standard obstetric care is episodic rather than continuous.
attributes.productDescription: Midwife-led maternity and gynecology clinics offering continuous, insurance-covered care from conception through postpartum, combining in-person visits with virtual-care extension.

founders.name: Anu Sharma
founders.background: 15+ years in healthcare innovation; founded Millie after a 2019 postpartum health crisis (preeclampsia and HELLP syndrome) following her own delivery.

founders.name: Talia Borgo
founders.background: Practicing midwife who served as Sharma's own midwife during her pregnancy; leads Millie's clinical model.

founders.name: Sarah Reynolds
founders.background: Co-founded Millie's technology platform alongside Sharma and Borgo.

name: Fay
description: Marketplace connecting patients with registered dietitian nutritionists for insurance-covered nutrition counseling, automating insurance billing for independent dietitians. Reports 1,000+ dietitians and partnerships with seven-plus national payers. Raised a $50m Series B at a disclosed $500m post-money valuation in February 2025, which this batch treats as an explicit Series-B exception to the roster's Series A hint.

disruptionMechanisms: Insurance-billing automation removing a practice-economics barrier
attributes.goToMarket: Payer partnerships plus direct-to-consumer, provider-supply-side recruiting
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software marketplace with licensed provider network
attributes.problemStatement: Independent dietitians face a major economic barrier in insurance billing, limiting the supply of accessible, insurance-covered nutrition care despite well-established clinical value.
attributes.productDescription: Insurance-covered dietitian marketplace giving patients $0-copay in-network nutrition counseling and giving independent dietitians practice-building tools, scheduling, and automated insurance billing.

founders.name: Sammy Faycurry
founders.background: Built the platform initially for his mother and sister, both registered dietitians.

founders.name: Mark Stefanski
founders.background: Co-built Fay's marketplace and insurance-billing technology.

name: Sohar Health
description: API for real-time healthcare insurance eligibility determination and verification of benefits, reporting a median 6-second response time, 96% benefits-data accuracy, and more than 5 million verification checks processed annually. Named customers include Talkiatry, Rula, Headway, Mindful Health Solutions, and Two Chairs.

disruptionMechanisms: Developer-first API replacing legacy clearinghouse workflows
attributes.goToMarket: Developer-led, direct sales to digital-health platforms
attributes.problemStatement: Legacy eligibility-verification clearinghouses are slow and inaccurate, causing claim denials, billing surprises, and lost patient conversion, especially in complex specialties like behavioral health.
attributes.productDescription: Real-time eligibility-verification API covering coverage discovery, benefits detail, in-network status, and cost estimation, with bulk API support for up to 1,000 checks per call.

founders.name: Ashish Mandavia
founders.background: Physician co-founder; company financing release states Sohar was founded by a physician and an engineer in 2023.

name: Tandem Health
description: European ambient AI clinical-documentation platform, CE-marked as a Class IIa medical device under EU MDR, generating structured clinical notes with coding suggestions and clinical decision support across 5,000+ care organizations in the Nordics, UK, Germany, France, and Spain. Distribution partnership with Accurx gives 200,000+ NHS professionals platform access.

regulatoryLabels: EU MDR Class IIa medical device
attributes.goToMarket: Direct enterprise sales plus distribution partnerships (Accurx/NHS)
attributes.problemStatement: European clinicians face significant documentation burden, and regulatory requirements (EU MDR) create a compliance barrier that most AI scribe vendors have not cleared, limiting trustworthy options in regulated markets.
attributes.productDescription: CE-marked ambient AI scribe generating structured clinical notes with ICD-10/SNOMED coding suggestions and source-linked clinical decision support, integrated with 100+ EHR systems across five European markets.

founders.name: Lukas Saari
founders.background: Named as CEO and co-founder in Sifted's Series A coverage; additional background unconfirmed in sources reviewed.

name: Synthpop
description: AI platform automating administrative workflows (fax processing, field extraction, order validation, AI outbound calling) for durable medical equipment suppliers and independent diagnostic testing facilities. Reports processing more than 2 million patients and integrating with eight major EHR systems.

founderArchetypes: Repeat digital-health founder with prior exit
disruptionMechanisms: Administrative-cost automation in a narrow regulated vertical
attributes.goToMarket: Direct enterprise sales to DME/IDTF operators
attributes.problemStatement: DME suppliers and diagnostic facilities operate under highly variable, payer-specific coverage rules that require extensive manual administrative processing, driving up cost and slowing patient access to equipment and testing.
attributes.productDescription: AI products (Fax Wrangler, Field Extractor, Order Validation, and a beta AI Caller) that automate referral intake, records analysis, payer-rule order validation, and payer-facing calls for DME and diagnostics providers.

founders.name: Elad Ferber
founders.background: Previously founded Spry Health, acquired by Itamar Medical (Nasdaq: ITMR) in 2021; prior digital-health and machine-learning-product experience.

founders.name: Jan Jannink
founders.background: Co-founded imeem and VoiceBase; held multiple CTO roles; taught AI courses at Stanford's computer science department.

name: Legion Health
description: AI-native, direct-to-consumer telepsychiatry practice offering psychiatric evaluation and medication management for conditions including ADHD, depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, PTSD, OCD, and insomnia, covered by major commercial insurance. Claims AI automates 95% of the administrative work behind patient care. Correct domain: legionhealth.com (roster hint legionhealth.ai does not resolve).

founderArchetypes: Pivoted founding team (B2B marketplace to D2C care delivery)
disruptionMechanisms: AI-driven lower cost-to-serve care-delivery operating model
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with employed/contracted psychiatrist network
attributes.problemStatement: Access to psychiatric medication management is slow and expensive nationally, and existing scaled telepsychiatry operators have drawn regulatory scrutiny over prescribing practices, creating an opening for a more disciplined, AI-efficient operator.
attributes.productDescription: Telehealth psychiatric evaluation and medication-management platform delivered by board-certified psychiatrists, with an AI-native back office claimed to automate 95% of administrative work.

founders.name: Yash Patel
founders.background: Co-founded the company in 2022; led its 2024 pivot from a clinical-operations marketplace to a direct-to-consumer AI-native psychiatry practice, per Behavioral Health Business.

founders.name: Arthur MacWaters
founders.background: Co-founding member per Behavioral Health Business; current operating role unconfirmed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Daniel Wilson
founders.background: Co-founding member per Behavioral Health Business; current operating role unconfirmed in sources reviewed.
